title: After Wooing Trump With Deals, Pakistan Gets a Seat at the Table
summary: Steve Witkoff, a diplomatic envoy, used the Board of Peace to announce an agreement that could raze a Pakistan-owned Manhattan hotel. Now the country is involved in negotiating peace talks with Iran.

title: Mortgage Rates Jump Again as Iran War Effects Ripple Through Housing Market
summary: The average rate on a 30-year mortgage in the United States hit 6.38 percent, the fourth increase since the war began.

title: Europeans Worry Russia Is Preparing to Deliver Drones to Iran
summary: The drones are an improved version of a weapon that Iran sent to Russia for use in its war in Ukraine.

title: Trump Eyes White House Treaty Room for Latest Renovation Project
summary: President Trump is making new plans in one of the most significant renovations in the history of the White House.

title: Trump Administration Begins Investigations Into Three Medical Schools
summary: The Justice Department’s demands for admissions-related data from Stanford, Ohio State and the University of California, San Diego, represent a flex of federal power.

title: Four Problems for Trump in Birthright Citizenship Case
summary: The president must confront a 1952 federal law, the possibility that millions will lose their citizenships, stateless foundlings and a fluid future.

title: Juries Take the Lead in the Push for Child Online Safety
summary: A pair of verdicts held social media companies accountable for harming young users, highlighting a growing backlash as Congress struggles to pass legislation.

title: Is Big Tech Facing a Big Tobacco Moment?
summary: Back-to-back courtroom losses have put technology giants, including Meta and Google, in uncertain territory as they face lawsuits and bans on teen users.

title: The Social Media Addiction Trials: What to Know
summary: Landmark trials are testing a new legal strategy claiming that Meta, TikTok, Snap and YouTube caused personal injury through addictive products.

title: Olympic Committee Bars Transgender Athletes From Women’s Events
summary: The decision is the most significant since Kirsty Coventry was elected last year to serve as president of the I.O.C.

title: When a Not-So-Dark Knight and His Sidekick Saved a Wacky Gotham
summary: Joel Schumacher apologized for “Batman & Robin,” his corny 1997 superhero movie, but thanks to its ice puns and bat nipples, it’s since become an accidental parody worth howling at.

title: ICE Agents at Some Airports Begin Checking IDs in Security Lines
summary: It was unclear whether having agents helping with screening passengers would improve wait times. Some travelers expressed worries.

title: LaGuardia Airport Runway Reopens After Plane Crash Wreckage Is Removed
summary: Officials expect the airport in New York to be fully operational later on Thursday. One of its two runways had been closed since Sunday, when a jet hit a truck, killing two pilots.

title: In California’s Central Valley, Latino Voters Are Up for Grabs in 2026’s Elections
summary: As in other Hispanic areas of the country, voters shifted toward Republicans in 2024. But now the question is whether this was a blip more than a durable trend.

title: Wealthy Investors Target Foes of Clean Energy, Seeking Revenge
summary: Renewable energy leaders said their industry got “rolled” in President Trump’s tax bill. Now they’re fighting back, starting in Texas.

title: South Dakota Governor Signs Bill Requiring Citizenship Proof to Vote
summary: The law, which mirrors national Republican priorities, requires newly registered voters to show that they are U.S. citizens in order to cast a ballot in state or local races.

title: Savannah Guthrie Says 2 Ransom Notes About Her Mother Were Likely Genuine
summary: Her interview on the “Today” show came more than 50 days after her mother, Nancy Guthrie, was taken from her home near Tucson, Ariz.

title: The Clues Binance Missed That Led to Billions in Crypto Flowing to Iran
summary: The world’s largest crypto exchange is under fire after investigators found accounts moving $1.7 billion to entities linked to Iran. Clues about those accounts were in plain sight for over a year.

title: Roman Catholic Churches See a Surge of New Converts
summary: Bishops are trying to understand what’s behind the wave. People joining the church described their reasons as highly personal.

title: Saving Hermit Crabs by Breeding Them in the Suburbs
summary: Often treated as throwaway pets, hermit crabs can live 50 years. Mary Akers, a self-taught expert, wants people to appreciate them as much as she does.

title: Local Opposition Is Slowing A.I. Data Centers. Wall Street Has Noticed.
summary: Tech companies are running into resistance from neighbors and may not be able to build at the pace they promised investors.

title: Seeking a Sounding Board? Beware the Eager-to-Please Chatbot.
summary: A new study of popular AI models shows that their feedback on social situations is far from impartial.

title: How Can America Be So Miserable When It’s So Rich?
summary: The just resentments of a shrinking middle class.

title: It’s Not Trump. It’s America.
summary: The country’s faith in its ability to shape the world to its liking is a much older malady.

title: Zelensky Says U.S. Is Conditioning Ukraine’s Security Guarantees on Donbas Surrender
summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said that President Trump “still chooses a strategy of putting more pressure on the Ukrainian side.”

title: The Kennedy Center Is Giving Bill Maher the Mark Twain Prize
summary: The annual award that recognizes merit in humor is going to the satirist and talk show host who has been critical of President Trump and political correctness.

title: Dash Crofts, of the Soft-Rock Duo That Sang ‘Summer Breeze,’ Dies at 87
summary: Seals & Crofts had a number of Billboard Top 20 songs in the 1970s before their chart topper brought them global fame.

title: Oscars Will Move to a Venue Outside Hollywood
summary: The event will be held in downtown Los Angeles starting in 2029 as part of a deal between the academy and AEG, a developer of entertainment venues.
